incident_objectives_and_command_emphasis.objectives:
1. Life Safety: Ensure the safety and accountability of all responders and protect the public in the Riverbend Industrial Park and adjacent residential neighborhoods. Maintain continuous PAR (personnel accountability reports) at least every 30 minutes in the hot and warm zones, and immediately after any significant event (e.g., structural collapse, explosion, or change in fire behavior).

2. Fire Suppression: Confine and control the fire within the existing footprint of Buildings 3 and 4 of the Riverbend Chemical Logistics Warehouse complex during this operational period. Prevent extension to Building 5 (solvent storage) and the rail spur to the south by establishing and maintaining defensive master stream operations and cooling exposures.

3. Hazardous Materials Containment: Identify, isolate, and control releases of hazardous materials, with priority focus on isopropyl alcohol, sodium hypochlorite, and flammable aerosols known to be stored on site. Coordinate with HazMat Group to establish product identification, air monitoring, and appropriate control zones (hot, warm, cold) and ensure decontamination procedures are in place and followed.

4. Evacuation and Public Protection: Maintain the existing 0.5-mile evacuation zone to the east and south of the incident and the shelter-in-place order for the Riverbend Apartments and Riverside Elementary School. Work with Law Enforcement Branch and Emergency Management to ensure road closures, perimeter control, and timely public information updates every 60–90 minutes or as conditions change.

5. Responder Health and Safety: Implement and enforce a formal Rehab Group with medical screening (vital signs, hydration assessment, and symptom checks) for all responders operating in hot and warm zones. No responder shall operate in an IDLH environment without a partner, proper PPE, and a functioning radio. Enforce maximum 20-minute work cycles on air and mandatory rehab between entries.

6. Environmental Protection: In coordination with the state environmental agency and city utilities, prevent contaminated runoff from entering the Riverbend Creek and municipal storm sewer system by deploying booms, diking, and diversion where feasible. Document all observed or suspected environmental impacts (e.g., fish kills, visible sheen, discolored runoff) and report them through the Environmental Unit.

7. Utilities and Infrastructure Protection: Coordinate with power, gas, and rail representatives to ensure utilities are safely controlled or shut down as needed. Maintain a safe standoff distance from overhead power lines and pressurized gas lines. Protect the main BNSF rail line and adjacent highway overpass from radiant heat and secondary explosions.

8. Investigation and Documentation: Preserve evidence to support a later origin-and-cause investigation by limiting unnecessary overhaul in identified origin areas once life safety objectives are met. Ensure all Divisions/Groups submit end-of-operational-period unit logs (ICS 214) and that photographs or video of key operational benchmarks and conditions are captured and archived by the Documentation Unit.

9. Planning for Transition: Develop contingency plans for potential transition from offensive to strictly defensive operations should structural integrity deteriorate further or if additional hazardous materials are involved. Prepare for potential unified command expansion if federal partners (EPA, ATF) assume a greater role.

10. Community and Stakeholder Coordination: Maintain ongoing communication with facility management, nearby businesses, and displaced residents. Ensure that the Joint Information Center (JIC) has accurate, up-to-date information, including road closures, air quality monitoring results, and anticipated duration of evacuations or shelter-in-place orders.
incident_objectives_and_command_emphasis.operational_period_command_emphasis:
Primary emphasis for this operational period is responder and public safety, strict adherence to HazMat protocols, and preventing fire spread to Building 5 and the rail spur.

All company officers will:
- Conduct continuous risk/benefit analysis: we will not risk firefighter lives for property that has already been lost.
- Enforce full structural PPE with SCBA in hot and warm zones. No exceptions. Anyone found operating without appropriate PPE will be removed from the hazard area and reported to Safety.
- Maintain clear, concise radio communications on assigned tactical channels, using plain language and identifying division/group and unit with each transmission.

Command Priorities:
1. Confirm and maintain collapse zones around Buildings 3 and 4. No personnel or apparatus are to be positioned within the marked collapse zones except for brief, supervised operations approved by Operations.
2. Maintain defensive master stream operations on the B/C corner of Building 4 to cool exposures and minimize extension to Building 5 and the rail spur tank cars. Monitor for any signs of BLEVE (Boiling Liquid Expanding Vapor Explosion) potential and immediately notify Operations and Safety if conditions change (flame impingement on tanks, venting, or audible changes).
3. HazMat Group will continue air monitoring for VOCs, oxygen levels, carbon monoxide, and potential chlorine or other irritant gases at the perimeter of the hot zone, downwind residential areas, and at Riverbend Creek. Results will be relayed to the Environmental Unit and Public Health for protective action decision-making.
4. Law Enforcement Branch and Traffic Control will keep the perimeter secure, prevent spontaneous re-entry into the evacuation zone by residents or business owners, and maintain clear ingress/egress routes for emergency vehicles.
5. All divisions will report PAR to Operations every 30 minutes and immediately following any Mayday, evacuation signal, or major change in conditions. Any missing or non-responsive personnel will be treated as a Mayday until located and verified safe.
6. Rehab Group will strictly enforce rest, hydration, and medical monitoring. Any responder with abnormal vital signs, signs of heat stress, respiratory symptoms, or dizziness will be evaluated by medical personnel and may be removed from operations for the remainder of the shift if indicated.
7. Units will minimize water usage where tactically feasible to reduce contaminated runoff while still achieving confinement and exposure protection. Environmental Unit will coordinate placement of booms and absorbent materials around storm drains and along Riverbend Creek.

Key Message to All Personnel:
"Slow is smooth, and smooth is fast." We are dealing with an active industrial fire involving mixed hazardous materials. Conditions can change rapidly. There is no tolerance for freelancing. All personnel must operate within the ICS structure, follow the chain of command, and immediately communicate any unexpected conditions, structural changes, or unusual odors or vapors.

Any questions about assignments, safety concerns, or resource needs are to be communicated promptly through Division/Group Supervisors to Operations. If you see something that does not look right, speak up immediately—everyone is responsible for safety.

site_safety_plan.approved_site_safety_plan_s_located_at: Hard copy located at Incident Command Post (ICP) Planning Section tent, Site Safety Plan binder, Tab 3; electronic copy stored on county EOC shared drive under /Incidents/2025_Riverbend_Warehouse_Fire/Safety/ICS208_SiteSafetyPlan_OpPeriod2.pdf.

incident_action_plan_components.other_attachments_1: Facility pre-incident plan and site diagram provided by Riverbend Chemical Logistics (rev. 2024-09).
incident_action_plan_components.other_attachments_2: Air monitoring summary and preliminary plume modeling from County Public Health and State Environmental Agency.
incident_action_plan_components.other_attachments_3: Evacuation and shelter-in-place zone maps, including traffic control points and detour routes.
incident_action_plan_components.other_attachments_4: Utility shutoff and rail coordination summary (power, gas, and BNSF rail line notifications and status).

approvals.prepared_by_position_title: Planning Section Chief, Riverbend Warehouse Fire
